question_title: Will Amazon Leo (formerly Project Kuiper) report a launched mission 10?
summary: Amazon Leo mission 10 (LA-06) is officially scheduled for NET April 27, 2026 on a ULA Atlas V from Cape Canaveral, confirmed by Amazon's official page, ULA, and NextSpaceflight. The resolution window is April 24-27. The launch was originally planned for April 24 but slipped to April 27 — the LAST day of the window. 8 of 9 previous Amazon Leo missions launched on schedule, and Amazon faces enormous FCC deadline pressure (must deploy ~1,618 satellites by July 30, 2026). However, April 27 being the final valid day means any weather scrub (~30-40% probability at CCAFS in late April) or technical issue would push the launch outside the window. The previous mission LA-05 was delayed 6 days due to weather. The "pending range approval" status adds further uncertainty. Balancing the strong schedule/incentive factors against the zero-margin risk yields ~65% probability.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.65 (65% YES)**

**Key findings:**

- **Mission 10 (LA-06)** is officially scheduled for **NET April 27, 2026** on a ULA Atlas V from Cape Canaveral — confirmed by Amazon's official page, ULA's website, and NextSpaceflight
- **Resolution window** is April 24–27 (after April 23, before April 28)
- April 27 is the **last valid day** — any scrub pushes outside the window

**Evidence favoring YES (~65%):**
- Launch is officially scheduled within the window
- 8 of 9 previous missions launched on their exact scheduled date
- Amazon faces massive FCC regulatory pressure (must deploy ~1,618 satellites by July 30, 2026; currently ~1,316 short)
- Atlas V is an extremely mature, reliable vehicle
- Could potentially launch earlier (originally targeted April 24)

**Evidence favoring NO (~35%):**
- Zero margin: April 27 is the last day — a 1-day slip means NO
- Weather scrub probability at Cape Canaveral in late April: ~30-40% per attempt
- Already slipped from April 24 to April 27 (constraints are real)
- LA-05 was delayed 6 days due to weather (March 29 → April 4)
- "Pending range approval" — date not yet fully locked

resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if Amazon announces a Leo satellite mission has launched. after April 23, 2026 and before April 28, 2026 at its Amazon Leo mission updates at https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/innovation-at-amazon/project-kuiper-satellite-rocket-launch-progress-updates
fine_print: The question may also resolve according to [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
